verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 is a type II single-pass TM glycoprotein resident primarily in the Golgi apparatus, where it catalyzes beta-1,4-galactosylation. The TM domain (N-terminal signal-anchor) retains it in the Golgi under baseline conditions. However, B4GALT1 is well-documented to also appear on the cell surface: a plasma-membrane form has been detected on sperm, mammary epithelial cells, and lymphocytes, where it participates in cell-surface lactosylation and signal transduction (e.g., EGF-receptor interactions). Additionally, a soluble secreted form circulates in serum after proteolytic shedding of the stem region. The surface display is therefore real but restricted to specific cell types/states, qualifying as contextual dual/tissue-restricted localization rather than ubiquitous surface presence. No broad clinical surface-targeting program is noted.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Extent and universality of PM pool varies by cell type; the dominant compartment is Golgi but documented PM display in lymphocytes/sperm/epithelial cells supports contextual surface accessibility.
